title: California Statewide Homelessness NGO Review Value Profile
target_universe: California homelessness-service nonprofit candidates selected from statewide awards, payment exposure, official adverse records, source opacity, and citation-ready public-record signals
language_rules: Treat public output as source-cited review leads, not legal findings.
Use official legal terms only when an official source supports the term.
Preserve named-party distinctions between entity findings, connected-party charges, and third-party transaction records.
Describe possible waste, fraud, abuse, mismanagement, or off-scope use as a review hypothesis until a human validates the underlying records.
target_discovery_rules: Seed from statewide grant, payment, and official adverse-record sources where available.
Add mandatory official adverse-record seeds even when payment exposure is lower.
Rank by Review Value Score, not by dollars alone.
Treat county-level missing contract, monitoring, and outcome records as source-access blockers until a portal hit, verified no-public-record search, or records-request blocker is documented.

forensic_tests.question: Do state and county payments map to contract purpose, invoices, deliverables, monitoring files, and provider-attributable outcomes for the same period?
forensic_tests.failure_mode: payment exposure without contract allowability and outcome support
forensic_tests.human_next_step: Pull state award records, county contracts, invoices, deliverables, monitoring letters, corrective actions, and provider-attributable outcome records for the entity.

forensic_tests.question: Do California charity and business registry records support the entity status, officers, addresses, and standing used in the investigation?
forensic_tests.failure_mode: entity identity or status not independently verified
forensic_tests.human_next_step: Verify California Attorney General charity filings and Secretary of State business records for names, status, officers, addresses, and filing dates.

forensic_tests.question: Do campaign, lobbying, website, or social records create a funding-scope or cost-allocation review question?
forensic_tests.failure_mode: off-scope activity signal without funding nexus
forensic_tests.human_next_step: Compare campaign/lobbying/public-statement records to contract scope, cost-allocation records, and allowable-use language.

human_action_rules: Prioritize official adverse records, audit findings, entity-status mismatches, and payment-to-deliverable gaps.
Every next step must name the document family a human should obtain.
Do not call a no-record public search clearance.
For statewide cases, separate statewide source blockers from county-specific blockers so a reviewer can see what can be automated and what still requires local records access.
